methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Portal Biotech is a Series A–stage biotechnology company founded by Andy Heron and Giovanni Maglia, having raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of comparable public companies and identifiable private peers within the biotechnology and health sector, limiting triangulation to single-method assessment.

The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $35M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $70M. This multiplier reflects typical capital step-ups from Series A to subsequent rounds in biotechnology. The method assumes that cumulative capital deployed reasonably approximates company value at this stage, a standard assumption when company-specific financial metrics are unavailable.

Given the single-method framework, the triangulated valuation range of $60M to $80M (with $70M as median) should be interpreted with caution. The low-end estimate of $60M and high-end of $80M reflect modest variance around the core stage-progression output rather than convergence across independent methods. Key uncertainties include the absence of peer valuation benchmarks, lack of disclosed revenue or burn metrics, and unknown intellectual property maturity. Any Series A valuation in this sector should be stress-tested against clinical development stage, IP defensibility, and team track record before final pricing.
